topic: Cost of Sales and Inventory Management
title: COGS: The cost of goods sold (COGS) is calculated by subtracting...
summary: Calculate cost of goods sold using the formula: beginning inventory plus purchases minus ending inventory.
prompt: The cost of goods sold (COGS) is calculated by subtracting ending inventory from the cost of goods available for sale.
solution: The formula for calculating COGS is precisely that: COGS = Cost of Goods Available for Sale - Ending Inventory. This calculation is crucial for determining the expenses associated with the goods sold during a period.
